summary:
Minocycline is known to induce characteristic flagellate-like skin pigmentation; no other mentioned medication has been associated with such distinctive cutaneous changes. Agent1 reasoned that among the given options, only minocycline was directly linked through medical literature to producing 'flagellate' or whip-like pigmentations on the skin due to its unique mechanism affecting melanin deposition. Therefore, it concluded by selecting \boxed{B}. This summary captures both core aspects required—identification of which drug fits based on provided information and exclusion of alternatives—and stays within two concise statements while emphasizing critical details pertinent to answering why one choice stands out over others regarding causative factors leading to particular types of dermatological manifestations like those seen here termed ‘flagellation’.
